public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as clerk of arrivals.
manner: Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Cosmouth, that reads as someone maritime, urbane, and commercially fluid, balancing archive-minded memory with port pragmatism, and the habits of a clerk of arrivals still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of clerk of arrivals in and around Cosmoria, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Cosmoria workable through the obligations of clerk of arrivals while managing the immediate pressure of storm weather in Cosmouth.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Cosmoria, that usually means carrying storm weather, trade vulnerability, archive and tax discipline without letting the role of clerk of arrivals visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Tideon Anchorlight can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.
Tideon Anchorlight needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Cosmoria.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Tideon Anchorlight communicates in public. In Cosmoria, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Cosmouth.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Cosmoria: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
